about_employee:
I am an experienced Audio Engineer and QA Automation specialist with over eight years across Intel, Amazon, Samsung, Logitech, and Luxoft, covering the full product lifecycle—from research and prototyping to deployment and post-launch refinement. In recent years, I have expanded my expertise into AI technologies, including large language model (LLM) agents, Databricks, MCP server infrastructure, and web services - both frontend and backend.

My core strengths lie in offline audio signal processing, perceptual and objective audio quality evaluation (like ETSI standards, using tools like ACQUA and HEAD batch processor), and writing automation tools using Python, MATLAB. I am highly experienced in creating test frameworks, processing large audio datasets, analysing results, and designing reliable automation solutions. I also have an experience in data processing (notably audio files) for ML trainings.

While I can contribute to real-time C++ audio systems, my strongest value is in research, offline processing, test development, and system validation. Although, I am able to work in C++ and learn fast an evironment.

I began my career at Intel in 2017, where I spent four years validating ADSP systems as a QA Engineer. This role gave me a strong foundation in Python-based test automation, CI/CD workflows, and robust testing methodologies. Also, later on I trained and led junior developers in this field. 

Being still Intel employee, I moved from ADSP firmware team to Intel Audio Labs where I spent the best time of my professional life. I pursued audio-focused R&D, working in controlled acoustic environments and developing tools and frameworks for audio evaluation. Our tests were part of the EVO certification for Intel platforms.

In 2023, I moved to Samsung Electronics as a Senior NLP Engineer to work as Audio Engineer in Data Processing team. I gained valuable experience in voice and AI technologies. I also served as a scrum master, training in agile methodologies. 

Since 2024 I have worked at Luxoft—first on a Logitech audio research project (loudspeakers playback, listening tests, audio database creation, and real-time C++ development). Last year, being a Luxoft employee, I worked on a DXC project focused on LLM infrastructure and agent integration. For example, we were delivering DXC AI Playground - an application used in real time by several hundred employees at once.

I thrive in distributed, international teams and contribute both technically and organisationally. I value good work culture, which for me means clear and open communication, genuine collaboration, effective Scrum, a friendly and supportive atmosphere, and clean, human-readable code. My attention to detail has been especially valuable in QA engineering, helping me detect subtle issues and improve test stability and clarity.

Outside of work, I am an active lindy hop dancer and teacher, and I enjoy sports, music, games, films, and literature.

about_employee:
After working in IT for half of my life I would say that
- Business goals come first. Hardware/Software is just a tool for reaching those goals. And one of my talents is to ask all the necessary questions to define those goals (problems) in a way IT can solve.
- Non-functional requirements define the right Architecture, while ensuring the business goals are reached. And I make sure that Architecture we build takes into account all the Quality Attribute Requirements, Life-cycle requirements and Constraints.
- Being Agile is key, both in Business and in IT. And I know exactly what Architectural decisions we need to take up front and what could be postponed so that Architecture and Implementation does not go separate ways while being Agile.
- Work is accomplished by Teams. While I can write the same amount of code as a group of developers, the mentoring, knowledge transfer and code review I do brings much more value.
- Maintenance requires much more efforts than development. That's why I make sure that Modifiability is defined as a requirement and properly addressed, including by adhering to Cloud Native Microservice Achitecture principles.
- Business is ever evolving and so are the IT solutions. By constantly keeping up-to-date with trends I ensure that multiple alternative solutions are evaluated for a business problem besides nowadays ubiquitous "Let's build Microservices with Kafka and Kubernetes".
- And all of the above uses Effective Communication as a foundation. I strongly believe in the power of open peer to peer communication on adhoc basis. And that is what I build and foster. Instead of numerous repeatedly scheduled meetings that devour enormous number of work hours bringing little in value.
